metadata.description: A storage class — the named policy by which a platform provisions storage volumes. Models the construct that Kubernetes calls a StorageClass: a provisioner, a reclaim policy, volume binding mode, and provider-specific parameters (IOPS, encryption, replication). Storage.Volume references this type to declare what kind of storage it needs; placement and enrichment policies select the right storage class for a request based on its capabilities. Provider-specific because storage class semantics and parameters vary across provisioners.

adopts.role: named storage provisioning policy (provisioner, reclaimPolicy, volumeBindingMode, parameters)

lifecycle.decommission: Provider deletes the storage class. Existing volumes provisioned by this class are not affected — they retain their storage; new provisioning against this class is no longer possible.

name.description: The storage class name (e.g. 'ceph-rbd-fast', 'gp3-encrypted'). Unique within the cluster.

provisioner.description: The storage provisioner that handles volumes of this class (e.g. 'rbd.csi.ceph.com', 'ebs.csi.aws.com').

reclaim_policy.description: What happens to the underlying storage when a volume is released.

volume_binding_mode.description: When volume binding and provisioning occur — immediately at PVC creation or deferred until a consumer is scheduled.

allow_volume_expansion.description: Whether volumes of this class can be expanded after creation.

capabilities.description: Queryable capability advertisements for placement policies.

parameters.description: Provider-specific parameters passed to the provisioner (e.g., pool name, fsType, compression). Opaque to UDLM — the provisioner interprets them.

name.description: The realized storage class name — the string value that Storage.Volume.storage_class references.

relationships.name: storage_backend
relationships.description: The storage cluster that backs this class (e.g., a Ceph cluster, a NetApp filer).
